title: Promotion gate implementation has drifted away from the composed gate contract
evidence: gates.py defines D1-D5 gate functions but promote.py does not call them
dossier generation omits allow_incomplete and sidecar path fields
current_state: The live promote path enforces V1-V9 errors, V4 coverage, and V7 incompleteness, but it does not consistently enforce editorial blocking, freshness, field normalization, absorbed-content blocking, or taxonomy-based ratification.
impact: Canon-facing promotion behavior is weaker than the documented gate contract, which creates governance and release-readiness risk.
recommendation: Refactor promote.py to delegate to the gate module and expand dossier serialization to match the expected promotion evidence schema.

title: osb_extract.py is half-migrated between dict records and typed records
evidence: osb_extract.py still accesses verses and markers with dict subscripting
current_state: The parser code path and the typed test fixtures disagree on record shape for verses and marker traces.
impact: Parser refactors are currently brittle and marker provenance behavior cannot be trusted as fully migrated.
recommendation: Choose one stable internal record model for extraction state and convert the parser, output builder, and tests fully to that model.

title: Common helper APIs have drifted from their test and caller contract
evidence: book_meta() returns None for missing codes while tests expect ValueError
page_ranges() returns a dict-shaped payload rather than tuple ranges
frontmatter.py exposes update_frontmatter_field() but not update_frontmatter()
discover_staged_books() and discover_staged_paths() now assume a filesystem path
current_state: The shared utility layer is between API shapes and has not finished the compatibility or test migration.
impact: Higher-level scripts and future agents are more likely to misread helper semantics or cargo-cult stale usage.
recommendation: Treat the common layer as an explicit compatibility cleanup lane and either restore the old contract or update all tests and callers in one pass.

title: Live artifact surfaces can still mislead if read in isolation
evidence: git diff -- reports/2JN_promotion_dossier.json shows a local registry_version bump to 1.4.0
WIS remains fully clean in dashboard and dossier state while staged editorial candidates still total 2
EST remains the OT lock blocker even though promoted artifacts exist
current_state: The boards capture the nuance, but single-surface reads can overstate cleanliness or readiness.
impact: A future agent or hurried operator could mistake promoted or dry-run dossier state for full project closure.
recommendation: Keep routing surfaces explicit about canon truth versus staged/editorial truth and add a lightweight stale-surface check into periodic ops review.

title: The root AGENTS contract is strong but over-centralized for Codex runtime use
evidence: Root AGENTS.md currently carries architecture, ownership, policy, workflow, validation, extraction, and external-agent rules
OpenAI Codex AGENTS guidance supports layered root and nested instruction files
current_state: The repo has a constitutional root document but no nested instruction stack for parser, promote, staging, or research subtrees.
impact: Codex can follow the current repo, but it has to carry a large prompt burden and cannot benefit from local overrides near specialized work.
recommendation: Keep the root AGENTS file as the constitution and add nested AGENTS files for parse, promote, staging, and research surfaces.

title: The project is ready for Codex skills, but only after code contracts stabilize
evidence: Recurring workflows already exist for book audit, promotion gate review, NT stabilization triage, and Phase 3 contract work
OpenAI Codex skills support reusable instruction bundles with progressive disclosure
current_state: The repo has the right ritual structure for skills but not yet the stable local task packets to support them cleanly.
impact: If skills are created too early, they will encode moving contracts and create more drift. If created after stabilization, they can materially reduce repeated context loading.
recommendation: Create a first skill set only after the promote/common/parse contract cleanup lands.
